title: Vector Addition with Magic Methods
description: Create a class called Vector with an __init__ method that takes x and y parameters and stores them as attributes. Implement __str__ to return '(x, y)' with the actual x and y values. Implement __add__ to return a new Vector whose x = self.x + other.x and y = self.y + other.y. Then create v1 = Vector(2, 3) and v2 = Vector(4, 5), and use print(v1 + v2) to show the result.
starter_code:
# Define your Vector class here

# Create vectors and print the result of addition

code_requirements.description: Defines a class using the class keyword
code_requirements.fail_message: You need to define a Vector class using the 'class' keyword.
Add an __str__ method so that print() shows the vector as (x, y).
Add an __add__ method that returns a new Vector with summed coordinates.

hints: Define __add__(self, other) to return Vector(self.x + other.x, self.y + other.y)
Create v1 = Vector(2, 3) and v2 = Vector(4, 5), then print(v1 + v2)
